title: Lazy merge driver self-registration on every tk command
description:
Add lazy driver self-registration so merge drivers are active in any checkout that has the tk binary, regardless of whether tk init was run.

The root cause of the original conflict: drivers are registered in .git/config (not versioned). A fresh clone or CI checkout has .gitattributes saying 'use tick/tick-activity drivers' but no driver definition → silent fallback to default 3-way merge.

Fix: add a CheckAndInstallMergeDrivers function and call it early in the command lifecycle.

Files to modify:
- internal/github/git.go — add CheckAndInstallMergeDrivers(repoRoot string) error:
  - Run 'git config --get merge.tick.driver' — if empty, call ConfigureMergeDriver
  - Skip gracefully if not in a git repo (return nil)
  - Cheap: one git config read, only writes if missing
- cmd/tk/cmd/root.go (or wherever PersistentPreRunE is set on rootCmd):
  - Add PersistentPreRunE that detects repo root and calls CheckAndInstallMergeDrivers
  - Must not break commands that run outside a git repo (tk version, tk help, etc.)
  - Skip for merge-file and merge-activity themselves (they're called BY git, not by users)

Tests in internal/github/:
- CheckAndInstallMergeDrivers: installs when missing, no-ops when already present
- Integration: simulate fresh clone (no .git/config entries), run any tk command, verify drivers are now configured

Run: go test ./internal/github/... ./cmd/tk/cmd/...
acceptance_criteria: Running any tk command in a repo without prior tk init registers the drivers; CheckAndInstallMergeDrivers is idempotent; go test ./... passes
closed_reason: Completed: CheckAndInstallMergeDrivers in git.go, PersistentPreRunE in root.go; 3 tests; duplicate helper fixed at integration
